concerns.text: Task T012 (Filter Hard Subset) depends on T002 (config.py) for `COMPLEXITY_THRESHOLD`, but T012 is listed as [P] (parallel-safe). If T002 is not completed before T012 starts, T012 will fail to read the threshold. Parallel tasks must not depend on the *execution* of other tasks in the same phase unless those tasks are purely declarative or the dependency is handled via file existence checks. Here, T012 reads a value from T002's output file. This is a hidden ordering dependency.

Task T021 (Data Locking) depends on T016 (Manual Human Inspection Gate). T016 is a manual gate that produces a flag file. T021 is marked [P] (parallel-safe) in the list, but it cannot run until the human creates the flag. Marking a task that waits on a manual human gate as [P] is misleading and violates the 'parallel-safe' definition (no hidden shared state or implicit ordering). It implies it can run concurrently with other Phase 3 tasks, which is impossible.

Tasks T022 (Static Baseline) and T023 (Iterative Agent) are marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, both depend on T021 (Data Locking) which copies the dataset. If T021 is not completed, T022/T023 will fail or read stale data. While they can run in parallel *with each other*, they cannot run in parallel with the *start* of Phase 4 until T021 is done. The [P] tag on T022/T023 is acceptable only if the dependency on T021 is explicit in the execution graph, but the current text implies they are independent of T021's completion status in a way that might confuse the runner.

Task T040 ('Run quickstart.md validation') is listed at the end of Phase N but has no clear producer artifact it validates against in the task list (e.g., it doesn't explicitly state it validates the output of T035). While T035 updates the file, T040's dependency on T035 is implied but not explicit in the task description. Clarify that T040 depends on T035 completion.
